_note: Permission model summarized from https://docs.frappe.io/framework/user/en/basics/users-and-permissions. Permissions are granted to ROLES per DocType via DocPerm rows, and users gain capabilities by holding roles. Standard ERPNext role names below are conventional defaults; exact bundled roles vary slightly by version.

permissionModel.rolesAndUsers: A User holds zero or more Roles (via Has Role child). A Role grants actions on DocTypes. Auto-roles: Guest (anonymous), All (every signed-up user), Administrator (superuser), Desk User (v15, system users with desk access).
permissionModel.permissionLevels: Each DocPerm row applies at a permlevel (default 0). Fields are tagged with a permlevel; a role needs permission at that level to read/write those fields. This enables field-level security (e.g. only managers can edit level-1 fields like rates/discounts).
permissionModel.userPermissions: Restrict a user to documents whose link field matches an allowed value (e.g. limit to a specific Company or Territory). Applied automatically across list/get/report.
permissionModel.docstatusPerms: submit/cancel/amend permissions gate the docstatus lifecycle; only roles with 'submit' can move docstatus 0->1, 'cancel' for 1->2, 'amend' to copy a cancelled doc into a new draft.
permissionModel.makerChecker: Maker-checker (segregation of duties) is achieved by combining (a) submit permission restricted to approver roles, and/or (b) a named Workflow whose transitions are gated by approver roles.
permissionModel.ifOwner: A DocPerm can be marked 'if_owner' so the permission only applies to documents the user created.
permissionModel.roleManager: Role Permissions Manager (and Customize Form) is the admin UI to view/override DocPerms; changes are stored as Custom DocPerm overrides.

roles.permissions: Full administrative access: manage users, roles, permissions, DocTypes, customization, system settings; effectively superuser short of Administrator.
Built-in single superuser; bypasses permission checks entirely.
Create and edit accounting transactions (draft/submit invoices, payments, journal entries); typically read on masters.
All Accounts User rights plus approval/cancel/amend, manage Chart of Accounts, fiscal year, period close, and accounting setup.
Create/submit stock transactions and view stock; manage items at operational level.
All Stock User rights plus warehouse setup, stock reconciliation, valuation settings, cancel/amend, item master management.
Create quotations and sales orders, manage customers/leads at operational level.
All Sales User rights plus approvals, pricing rules, price lists, territory/customer-group setup and sales analytics.
Create material requests, RFQs, supplier quotations and purchase orders; manage suppliers operationally.
All Purchase User rights plus PO approval/cancel, supplier-group setup and buying analytics.
Create/submit BOMs, work orders and job cards; run production operations.
All Manufacturing User rights plus workstation/operation/routing setup, BOM approval and production planning.
Manage projects, tasks and timesheets.
Create and resolve support tickets/issues, manage SLAs and customer communications.
Maintain employee records, attendance, leave applications and basic payroll inputs.
All HR User rights plus salary structures, payroll entry, leave policy/allocation, recruitment and appraisal.
Approve/reject leave applications routed to them (used in leave workflow).
Approve/reject expense claims (used in expense workflow).
Manage quality goals, procedures, reviews and inspection templates.
Manage the Item master, item groups, brands and item pricing.
Read-only/report access to financial data for review without edit rights.
Manage web pages, blogs, web forms and portal/website settings.
Baseline role auto-granted to every signed-up user; grants access to self-service portal docs and shared masters explicitly opened to it.
Anonymous/unauthenticated access; used for public website pages and web forms only.
